page: Microsoft Azure NC40ads_H100_v5 is a GPU-accelerated server instance belonging to the NCadsH100v5 family. It features 40 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V84 processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 320.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a local storage capacity of 3971 GB with Premium Storage capability. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 94 GB of VRAM.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in several areas. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core testing, Geekbench image processing, and PassMark memory scoring. It registers strong top 25% performance in multi-core CPU stress tests, memory bandwidth (both cached small block and uncached large block read/write), Geekbench single-core, and PassMark database operations. Large-model LLM inference text generation (70B model) operates at an average level of 3.88 tokens per second, while smaller models (7B and 135M) achieve strong top 25% generation speeds.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the NC40ads_H100_v5 consolidates high-density AMD EPYC processing, a massive local NVMe storage allocation, and a premier Hopper-class GPU into a single node. This resource density reduces the need for multi-node clustering for workloads that fit within a single 94 GB VRAM footprint. Appropriate workloads include large-scale database operations, image processing, multi-threaded data decompression, and medium-scale LLM inference or fine-tuning tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure NC40ads_H100_v5 is a GPU-accelerated server instance featuring 40 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V84 vCPUs operating at 3.7 GHz, 320.0 GB of system memory, and 3971 GB of local storage. It incorporates a single NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 94 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for single-core CPU execution, multi-threaded decompression, and memory performance, alongside strong top 25% rankings in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The hardware configuration delivers high resource density, combining substantial local storage with high-capacity GPU memory. This profile makes the instance highly effective for workloads such as database operations, image processing, and large language model text generation.

tagline: An accelerated server combining AMD EPYC processing, substantial local storage, and NVIDIA Hopper architecture for data-intensive workloads.

Single NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPU with 94 GB VRAM
